Bosnian mafia.description: The Bosnian mafia is the body of illegal gangs and criminal organizations operating in Bosnia and Herzegovina and within the Bosnian diaspora. Founded in the early 1990s, it is involved in drug trafficking, smuggling, extortion, and other crimes. Groups operate across Europe, South America, and North America, with revenues estimated between €600 million and €900 million. Corruption and weak inter-entity law enforcement cooperation hinder anti-mafia efforts.

'Ndrina Nicoscia.description: The 'Ndrina Nicoscia is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Isola Capo Rizzuto. It is allied with the Grande Aracri and Russelli clans and operates in several Italian regions, including Emilia-Romagna. The clan was heavily targeted in the 2000 operation Scacco Matto, leading to the arrest of its boss Salvatore Nicoscia. It has been involved in a feud with the Arena clan and is active in drug trafficking, extortion, and other crimes.

George Street.description: George Street is a Bloods-affiliated street gang based in Belize City, Belize, founded in the late 1980s by US deportee Heathcliff Reyes. It is one of the country's earliest gangs and has been involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs. The gang has been considered one of the most powerful in Belize, with membership estimated at up to 150 in 2019.

Inzerillo Mafia clan.description: The Inzerillo Mafia clan is a Sicilian Mafia clan based in Palermo, founded in the 1960s by Salvatore Inzerillo. It was once among the most powerful clans in Sicily, known as the 'aristocracy of Palermo's mafia.' The clan engaged in drug trafficking and money laundering, with strong ties to the Gambino crime family in the United States. After being nearly wiped out in the Mafia war of the early 1980s, the family fled to the US but later returned to Sicily in the 2000s and remains active.

descriptions: The text links the Buffalo family with the Inzerillo family in international drug-trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The investigation concluded with 200 arrested that linked the Sicilian Mafia families (Spatola, Gambino and Inzerillo) and the American Mafia families in Buffalo, New York City, Philadelphia, Chicago and San Francisco with international drug-trafficking.

descriptions: The Inzerillo family was a primary rival of the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corleonesi's primary rivals were Stefano Bontade , Salvatore Inzerillo and Tano Badalamenti , bosses of various powerful Palermo Mafia families.

Montenegrin mafia.description: The Montenegrin mafia refers to various criminal organizations based in Montenegro or composed of Montenegrins, active in narcotics smuggling, tobacco smuggling, and arms trafficking. The most prominent groups are the Kavač and Škaljari clans, which are central to the Balkan cartel. They have strong alliances with Italian mafia groups like the Camorra and Sacra Corona Unita. The mafia emerged in the post-Yugoslav era and is still active.

Young Lords.description: The Young Lords were a far-left political organization that evolved from a Chicago street gang founded in 1959. They conducted direct action campaigns and community service programs in Chicago and New York City, advocating for Puerto Rican independence and socialism. The group split into the Young Lords Party in 1970 and dissolved in 1972, with some members joining the Puerto Rican Socialist Party.

descriptions: Latin Eagles spawned the Young Lords as a political offshoot.
edges.evidence_quote: Famous for spawning a strong Latino political organization in the late 1960s called the Young Lords

descriptions: The Young Lords allied with the Black Panther Party.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the leadership of José "Cha Cha" Jiménez , who was inspired by civil rights leaders and the Black Panther Party , the Chicago Young Lords allied themselves with various socialist organizations.

descriptions: The Young Lords were inspired by and allied with the Black Panther Party.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the leadership of José "Cha Cha" Jiménez, who was inspired by civil rights leaders and the Black Panther Party, the Chicago Young Lords allied themselves with various socialist organizations.

descriptions: The Young Lords Party collaborated with HRUM to form the Think Lincoln Committee.
edges.evidence_quote: the YLP founded the "Think Lincoln Committee" (TLC) in collaboration with the Health Revolutionary Unity Movement (HRUM).

descriptions: The Young Lords collaborated with LADO in a building occupation.
edges.evidence_quote: In early 1969, the Young Lords occupied the Presbyterian McCormick Theological Seminary in collaboration with the Black Panthers, LADO, the Young Patriots Organization , and the SDS for almost a week

descriptions: The Movement Pro Independence criticized the Young Lords Party.
edges.evidence_quote: Established nationalist groups like the Movement Pro Independence (MPI), led by Juan Mari Brás, criticized their perceived arrogance and unfamiliarity with the local independence movement.

descriptions: Some YLP members left to join the PSPR.

Tokyo Boyz.description: Japanese American gang founded in 1942 at the Manzanar concentration camp during World War II. Initially formed by rebellious youths and adults in response to harsh internment conditions, the gang engaged in petty crimes like stealing rations. After internment, members continued criminal activities in California, with membership growing to over 500. The gang was active until 1996.

descriptions: Issei Boys is a sub-group of Tokyo Boyz.
edges.evidence_quote: They were split into three groups/ranks, The Kibei Boys (Japanese-Americans from Japan), Issei Boys (1st generation Japanese-Americans), and the Nisei Boys (2nd generation Japanese-Americans).

Tribesmen Motorcycle Club.description: The Tribesmen Motorcycle Club is a prominent outlaw motorcycle club based in the North Island of New Zealand, founded in 1980 in Murupara. They are known for their yellow colors and middle finger salute, and have chapters nationwide. The club has been involved in drug dealing, assault, and methamphetamine manufacturing. As of 2020, they are considered the dominant gang in Christchurch.

descriptions: Mongrel Mob and Tribesmen MC were both involved in meth labs in Murupara, but the text does not specify cooperation or conflict between them.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, members of the Murupara Mongrel Mob, and the Tribesmen MC Murupara chapters were involved in multiple methamphetamine laboratories that were raided by police.

descriptions: Killer Beez was founded as a feeder gang for the Tribesmen.
edges.evidence_quote: The Killer Beez were founded in 2003 by then-Vice President of the Tribesmen New Zealand, Josh Masters, as a feeder gang for the Tribesmen.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2020, 10 members of the Tribesmen Christchurch confronted a small group of Mongrel Mob members outside of the Christchurch District Court. This confrontation led to a fight occurring.

descriptions: Rebels attempted to recruit Tribesmen members through a patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2011, the newly established Rebels Motorcycle Club New Zealand Chapter voiced its intentions to 'patch over' the Tribesmen Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Ferraro-Gugliotta and Mazzagatti-Polimeni were opposing sides in the Faida di Oppido Mamertina.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Oppido Mamertina : dura sei anni, dal 1992 al 1998 e dopo una tregua riesplode nel 2011, e dovrebbe essersi conclusa nel novembre 2012 con l'operazione Erinni delle forze dell'ordine (novembre 2013). Da una parte sono schierati i Ferraro-Gugliotta e dall'altra i Mazzagatti - Polimeni

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Millwall Bushwackers.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C. , the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals , as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters , a firm associated with Chelsea F.C. , and the Millwall Bushwackers , a firm associated with Millwall F.C.
